title: US is not using Prestwick Airport to strike Iran, UK Government says

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Prestwick Airport is not being used by the US to launch military strikes, the UK Government has said

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: US is not using Prestwick Airport to strike Iran, UK Government says This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: PRESTWICK Airport is not being used by the US to launch military strikes, the UK Government has said. John Swinney had previously said he would "consider" banning US military aircraft if it was confirmed they were involved in strikes on Iran.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Prestwick Airport 'not being used by US to launch strikes', says UK Government
